decisions.title: Why Three Layers of Compression
decisions.description: Context compaction uses three strategies in sequence: (1) summarize old conversation turns into a brief recap, (2) drop tool call/result pairs that are no longer relevant, and (3) preserve the most recent turns verbatim. This layered approach retains the most important information — recent context and key decisions — while aggressively compressing older, less relevant history.
decisions.alternatives: A single summarization pass is simpler but loses structural information about tool calls. Keeping everything and relying on the model's attention mechanism wastes tokens and eventually hits the context limit. The three-layer approach provides graceful degradation of detail over time.

zh.title: 为什么采用三层压缩
zh.description: 上下文压缩依次使用三种策略：(1) 将旧的对话轮次总结为简短概要；(2) 丢弃不再相关的工具调用/结果对；(3) 完整保留最近的对话轮次。这种分层方法保留了最重要的信息——近期上下文和关键决策——同时积极压缩较旧的、不太相关的历史。

decisions.title: Why Not Just Truncate
decisions.description: Naive truncation (dropping the oldest messages) destroys critical context: the original user request, early decisions that shaped the current approach, and the system prompt's effectiveness. An agent that forgets why it started a task will either repeat work or pursue a different strategy mid-stream. Summarization preserves intent even as details are compressed.
decisions.alternatives: Sliding window truncation is the simplest approach and works for short tasks. For longer sessions, we considered a retrieval-based approach where old messages are stored in a database and retrieved on demand. This works but adds latency and complexity. Summarization strikes a practical balance.

zh.title: 为什么不能简单截断
zh.description: 朴素的截断（丢弃最旧的消息）会破坏关键上下文：原始用户请求、塑造当前方案的早期决策，以及系统提示的效力。一个忘记自己为什么开始任务的 Agent，要么重复工作，要么在执行过程中转向不同的策略。摘要总结在压缩细节的同时保留了意图。
